text:
The athletics schedule at the Paris 2024Olympicsis in full swing, with several stars having already taken gold in front of a boisterous Stade de France crowd.

Amongst them has been Julien Alfred, whoclaimed Saint Lucia’s first ever Olympic medalwith a stunning run to take gold in the women’s 100m final on Saturday night, and USA’s Noah Lyles,who won the closest men’s 100m Olympic final in history24 hours later.

Keely Hodgkinson has also been getting in on the golden act, finishing top of the podium with a sensational run to claim gold in the 800m.

And those watching back home on TV will have noticed the likes of Alfred, Lyles and Hodgkinson celebrating by ringing a bell in the stadium after their race.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

But why exactly do some athletes ring the bell at the end of their events?

Every gold medalist in the athletics is being given the honour of ringing the bell.

Inscribed with ‘Paris 2024’, the bell is situated on the edge of the track at the iconic venue.

The bell will eventually be relocated to Notre Dame, which will be its permanent home once the renovation of the cathedral has been completed.

The bell will be suspended in one of the cathedral’s bell towers following the conclusion of the Games, with the idea being that the sound of Olympic glory will forever be heard from the Notre Dame.

The Notre Dame Cathedral has been undergoing repair work since a fire damaged the building in 2019.

The bells inside Notre Dame have rung only twice since the blaze; first for the funeral of former French President Jacques Chirac in 2020, and then later that year to mark the first anniversary of the fire.

‘In a way, Paris 2024 is helping to rebuild Notre-Dame. A part of the Games and the Olympic spirit will remain in Notre-Dame for life,’ Pierre-André Lacout, a manager at the Stade de France, said.

Head of the Paris 2024 organising committee, Tony Estanguet, has hailed the bell-ringing as a way to engage supporters and as a way of incorporating Paris and its culture into every venue in use at the games.

‘It’s just for the gold medalists, and it’s a great way for them to celebrate,’ Estanguet said.

The first week of the Olympics saw the rugby sevens take centre stage at the Stade de France, with the players taking it in turns to ring the bell.

The triumphant French men’s team took great delight in ringing the bell after they secured the hosts’ first gold in the event in front of a partisan 70,000 capacity crowd with victory over double-reigning Olympic champions, Fiji.

title: Why do athletes ring the bell at the Paris 2024 Olympics? | Metro News
description: Why some athletes at the 2024 Paris Olympics are ringing the bell at the end of their events at the Stade de France.
